MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Sep-23-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, not contested, and tentative ruling adopted: Specially Appearing and Proposed Intervenor Great American Insurance Company's Motion to Vacate and Set Aside Default and Default Judgment is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Proposed Intervenor's request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibits A and B. (Evidence Code section 452(c).) The request for judicial notice is denied as to Exhibit S. The October 3, 2017 default judgment entered against dissolved corporation C.F. Bolster Company is set aside solely as related to Great American Insurance Company. The request to set aside the August 18, 2015 entry of default against C.F. Bolster Company is denied. Great American Insurance Company may not file an answer on behalf of C.F. Bolster Company. (see W. Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Ct., (2011) 199 Cal. App. 4th 1196, 1209.) Great American Insurance Company is granted leave to file an answer in intervention within 10 days of the entry of order on the instant motion. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND

Notice Of Continued Motion To Consolidate For Trial The Cases Against Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. And Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. Formerly Known As Kaiser Cement Corporation
Off Calendar Feb-19-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff.
Andrew Sharp of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendants Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.
CJ Manoli of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendants Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.
Laura Przetak of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel.

RULING: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE FOR TRIAL THE CASES AGAINST KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. AND HANSON PERMANENTE CEMENT, INC. FORMERLY KNOWN AS KAISER CEMENT CORPORATION
Parties stipulated to have all matters heard simultaneously. Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to consolidate for trial the cases against Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. formerly known as Kaiser Cement corporation, into the following subgroups: 1) wrongful death/mesothelioma; 2) living/lung cancer: 3) wrongful death/lung cancer; 4) living/other cancer; 5) wrongful death/other cancer; 6) living/asbestosis; 7) wrongful death/asbestosis; 8) living/pleural disease, is denied. The actions do not involve sufficient common questions of law or fact, economy and convenience would not be served by a joint trial, and consolidation may prejudice one or both of the defendants. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293 spham28@yahoo.com (Tel: 415-517-5439) (503/CML)

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendants Carroll Duncan Co., C.F. Bolster Company, Jack A Brown Sons Inc., And Marine Engineering And Supply Company
Off Calendar Oct-03-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Dept. 514, Judge: Gail Dekreon, Clerk: A. Mok. Proceedings reported by: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840.

James H. Ghilotti, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, Chris Votino, SR.
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.

No appearance by defaulted defendants CARROLL DUNCAN & CO., C.F. BOLSTER COMPANY, JACK A BROWN & SONS INC., AND MARINE ENGINEERING AND SUPPLY COMPANY.
Default of Defendants has been duly entered.

After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendants as follows:
IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that the plaintiffs shall recover damages from defendants CARROLL DUNCAN & CO., C.F. BOLSTER COMPANY, JACK A BROWN & SONS INC., AND MARINE ENGINEERING AND SUPPLY COMPANY in the amount of:

As to CARROLL DUNCAN & CO:
$92,500.00 in Economic Damages,
$750,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages,
Total Judgment of $842,500.00.

As to C.F. BOLSTER COMPANY:
$92,500.00 in Economic Damages,
$750,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages,
Total Judgment of $842,500.00.

As to JACK A BROWN & SONS INC.:
$92,500.00 in Economic Damages,
$750,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages,
Total Judgment of $842,500.00.

As to MARINE ENGINEERING AND SUPPLY COMPANY:
$92,500.00 in Economic Damages,
$750,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages,
Total Judgment of $842,500.00.

The Clerk is ordered to enter the judgment.
Judgment signed in open court.
